The '''Battle of Thila''' was a conflict in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], during which the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] base on [[Thila/Legends|Thila]] was destroyed.

==Background==
Sometime after the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] evacuation of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], [[Supreme Prophet]] [[Kadann]] led [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Afsheen Makati]] to the Rebel base on the planet [[Thila/Legends|Thila]]. While in orbit, Kadann overheard Makati's first officer mocking his glittering robes, the uniform of the [[Prophets of the Dark Side|Secret Order of the Emperor]]. He lashed out at Makati with Force Lightning and chided "Learn to rein in your subordinates, dear Admiral."

==The battle==
Prior to the battle, [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] had been readying [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles's]] [[T-47 airspeeder/Legends|T-47 airspeeder]]. Unfortunately, Wedge jumped the gun as the Imperials began their attack and launched his airspeeder, only to find, thanks to fellow pilot [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]], that the Wookiee had launched as well, hanging on to the speeder for dear life. During the fighting, Chewbacca leapt from Wedge's T-47 onto an enemy [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighter]], broke through the hatch and tossed the TIE pilot from his cockpit. The [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] then flew the fighter back to the hangar bay, following the completion of the battle. Despite the successful evacuation of Thila Base, Wedge found himself in the precarious situation of having both Chewbacca and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] notably irritated with him.

==Behind the scenes==
The chronological placement of this battle is uncertain. In writing ''[[Star Wars: Empire: The Wrong Side of the War]]'', [[Randy Stradley|Welles Hartley]] mistakenly jumped to the conclusion that the Thila base had remained completely unchronicled by the expanded universe (having only been mentioned in ''[[The Movie Trilogy Sourcebook]]''). Thus, one must infer that after the [[Ambush at Thila]], the Rebels must have decided that the last place the Imperials would ever look for the new Rebel base would be in a location where a previous Rebel base had been.
